QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_57

question.text: In the Indian forex market, what is the maximum validity period for exchange-traded currency futures contracts?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Exchange-traded currency futures in India are available for up to 12 monthly contracts. For USDINR, contracts are available for all 12 months. For cross-currency pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY), contracts are available for up to 12 months as well. **The Trap:** While the near-month contracts are most liquid, contracts extending to 12 months are available. The 3-month option might seem right for near-term hedgers but doesn't reflect the full contract availability. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch3 specifies currency futures contract tenors. Compare with equity F&O (3 monthly + weekly) and IR futures (multiple maturities). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Longer-dated currency futures have wider bid-ask spreads and lower liquidity — hedgers needing 6-12 month cover should consider the rollover cost of near-month contracts vs directly using far-month contracts.
